name: gsh-vision-tool
description: Language model tool for attaching screenshots as real image context during agent execution.

commands.title: GSH: Inspect Screenshot With Copilot Vision

chatParticipants.name: gsh-vision
chatParticipants.description: Analyzes screenshots with real image input. Supports 1–10 images per call.

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Analyze images. Usage: /analyze /path/to/img1.png :: /path/to/img2.png :: What to evaluate?

commands.name: analyze-video
commands.description: Analyze a video. Usage: /analyze-video /path/to/video.mp4 :: What to analyze?

languageModelTools.name: gsh-take-screenshot
languageModelTools.userDescription: Capture a screenshot on macOS. Returns the path to the saved PNG.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool to capture a screenshot of the screen, a specific application window, or a screen region on macOS. Returns the absolute path to the saved PNG file. Chain with analyze_images to inspect, compare, or evaluate the captured screenshot. Modes: 'fullscreen' (entire screen, default), 'window' (specific app window by name), 'region' (rectangle by x/y/width/height).

outputPath.description: Optional absolute path for the output PNG. Defaults to a timestamped file in the system temp directory.

mode.description: Capture mode. 'fullscreen' (default): entire screen. 'window': a specific window by app name. 'region': a rectangle defined by x, y, width, height.

appName.description: Application name whose frontmost window to capture (used with mode 'window'). Example: 'AIO Server'.

languageModelTools.name: gsh-analyze-images
languageModelTools.userDescription: Analyze up to 10 screenshots or frame captures in a single call.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool to analyze screenshots during visual development and testing. Accepts 1–10 image paths and a freeform goal. Use it for single-image inspection, before/after comparison, batch design evaluation, or any visual analysis. The images are sent as real image data to a vision model (Claude Sonnet 4.6 by default). Prefer this over reading screenshot files as text.

imagePaths.description: Absolute paths to image files (1–10). Order matters when comparing: reference images first.

goal.description: What to determine from the images. Be specific: inspect quality, compare differences, evaluate design, verify a fix, check layout, etc.

context.description: Optional extra context about the images.

style_context.description: Optional QSS/CSS stylesheet content for the page being viewed. Helps the model suggest concrete selector-level fixes.

languageModelTools.name: gsh_analyze_images
languageModelTools.userDescription: Analyze up to 10 screenshots or frame captures in a single call.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool to analyze screenshots during visual development and testing. Accepts 1–10 image paths and a freeform goal. Use it for single-image inspection, before/after comparison, batch design evaluation, or any visual analysis. The images are sent as real image data to a vision model (Claude Sonnet 4.6 by default). Prefer this over reading screenshot files as text.

imagePaths.description: Absolute paths to image files (1–10). Order matters when comparing: reference images first.

goal.description: What to determine from the images. Be specific: inspect quality, compare differences, evaluate design, verify a fix, check layout, etc.

context.description: Optional extra context about the images.

style_context.description: Optional QSS/CSS stylesheet content for the page being viewed. Helps the model suggest concrete selector-level fixes.

languageModelTools.name: gsh_take_screenshot
languageModelTools.userDescription: Capture a screenshot on macOS. Returns the path to the saved PNG.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool to capture a screenshot of the screen, a specific application window, or a screen region on macOS. Returns the absolute path to the saved PNG file. Chain with analyze_images to inspect, compare, or evaluate the captured screenshot. Modes: 'fullscreen' (entire screen, default), 'window' (specific app window by name), 'region' (rectangle by x/y/width/height).

outputPath.description: Optional absolute path for the output PNG. Defaults to a timestamped file in the system temp directory.

mode.description: Capture mode. 'fullscreen' (default): entire screen. 'window': a specific window by app name. 'region': a rectangle defined by x, y, width, height.

appName.description: Application name whose frontmost window to capture (used with mode 'window'). Example: 'AIO Server'.

languageModelTools.name: gsh-analyze-video
languageModelTools.userDescription: Analyze a video by extracting frames and running them through a vision model.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool to analyze video content. Extracts frames at regular intervals using ffmpeg, analyzes each batch with a vision model, and merges with auto-generated transcript (via local ASR or yt-dlp subtitles for URLs). Returns a structured JSON timeline with per-frame visual descriptions, OCR-like text extraction, and a human-readable report. Requires ffmpeg/ffprobe to be installed. Accepts local video paths or URLs (via yt-dlp). Supports time window restriction and custom sampling intervals.

videoPath.description: Absolute path to a local video file, or a URL (YouTube/Shorts) if yt-dlp is installed.

goal.description: What to analyze or determine from the video. Be specific about what visual evidence to look for.

startSec.description: Optional start time in seconds. Limits analysis to a time window.

sampleEverySec.description: Interval between frame samples in seconds. Auto-calculated if omitted.

maxFrames.description: Maximum number of frames to extract and analyze. Default: 30, max: 60.

includeReport.description: Include a human-readable report in the output. Default: true.

includeTimeline.description: Include the structured timeline segments in the output. Default: true.

autoTranscribe.description: Automatically transcribe the video audio using a local ASR backend (whisper, mlx_whisper, or whisper-cpp). Default: true.

whisperModel.description: Whisper model name for ASR. Default: 'base'. Larger models (small, medium, large) are more accurate but slower.

languageModelTools.name: gsh_analyze_video
languageModelTools.userDescription: Analyze a video by extracting frames and running them through a vision model.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool to analyze video content. Extracts frames at regular intervals using ffmpeg, analyzes each batch with a vision model, and merges with auto-generated transcript (via local ASR or yt-dlp subtitles for URLs). Accepts local video paths or URLs (via yt-dlp). Supports time window restriction and custom sampling intervals.
